LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. INTERVENORS SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS AND BRUCE HORTON'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE IN ACTION IS GRANTED. THE SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS AND BRUCE HORTON IN HIS CAPACITY AS PRESIDENT OF THE SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS HAVE MET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 387(A) AND (B). THE SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS AND BRUCE HORTON IN HIS CAPACITY AS PRESIDENT OF THE SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS HAVE ALSO DEMONSTRATED THAT THEY HAVE THE REQUISITE STANDING TO INTERVENE AND ARE ENTITLED TO INTERVENE AS A REAL PARTY IN INTEREST. (SEE MARKEN V. SANTA MONICA-MALIBU UNIFIED SCHOOL DIST. (2012) 202 CAL.APP.4TH 1250, 1267, FN. 13.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. calendar.matters:
Petitioner Pmsa'S Notice Of Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar May-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas R. Burke, Esq. of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP, Tel. 415.276.6552, and Conte C. Cicala, Esq. of Flynn Delich & Wise LLp, Tel. 415.693.5562, for plaintiff and moving party;
Christiana Tiedemann, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2218, for respondent and opposing party Board of Pilots.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PETITIONER PACIFIC MERCHANT SHIPPING ASSOCIATION's Motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Costs is Granted in part and denied in part. Denied as to Respondent Board of Pilot Commissioners (the "Board"). Petitioner Pacific Merchant Shipping Association ("PMSA") has not established that it prevailed against the Board. Granted as to Respondent Port Agent. The Court of Appeal held that the Port Agent is a public official subject to Public Record Act ("PRA") requests. (Board of Pilot Commissioners for the Bays of San Francisco, San Pablo and Suisin et al. v. Superior Ct. (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 577, 591, "We find that the Port Agent must be considered a state officer, at least when performing the official duties provided by statute or Board regulation.") Although the Court of Appeal held that PMSA did not establish that the "Pilot Logs" were public documents, PMSA "succeed[ed] on [a] significant issue in the litigation and achieve[d] some of the benefit sought in the lawsuit." (Garcia v. Governing Board of Bellflower Unified School District (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1058, 1065.) PMSA has presented evidence showing that prior to the litigation, the Port Agent responded to only one of five PRA requests, with the following disclaimer: "[T]his production of information is done without conceding that all of the materials provided are, in fact, subject to the obligations arising out of your request under the Public Records Act." (Ex. E to PMSA's original verified petition, attached as Exhibit A to the Port Agent's opposition brief.) After the litigation, the Port Agent produced voluminous documents in response to PMSA's requests. (Declaration of Michael Jacob, par. 5.) "Cases denying attorney fees to a plaintiff under the act have done so because substantial evidence supports a finding that the litigation did not cause the [agency] to disclose any of the documents ultimately made available'" (Los Angeles Times v. Alameda Corridor Transportation Authority (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 1381, 1391, citations omitted.) The evidence supports the finding that the Port Agent responded to PMSA's PRA requests once the Court of Appeal declared the Port Agent a public official. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Leave To Intervene In Action
Off Calendar Aug-08-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CONTE C. CICALA OF FLYNN, DELICH & WISE LLP, TEL. NO. 693-5562 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CARI COHORN OF PHILLIPS ERLEWINI & GIVEN, APPEARING FOR THE PROPOSED INTERVENORS SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS AND BRUCE HORTON.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. INTERVENORS SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOTS AND BRUCE HORTON'S motion For Leave To Intervene In Action is Granted. The San Francisco Bar Pilots and Bruce Horton in his capacity as President of the San Francisco Bar Pilots have met the requirements of CCP 387(a) and (b). The San Francisco Bar Pilots and Bruce Horton in his capacity as President of the San Francisco Bar Pilots have also demonstrated that they have the requisite standing to intervene and are entitled to intervene as a real party in interest. (See Marken v. Santa Monica-Malibu Unified School Dist. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1250, 1267, fn. 13.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
